title: Efficiency in Medicinal Chemistry via Computational Chemistry
author_string: Rajapriyan, Manojkumar, Meera Moydeen, A., Syed Ali Padusha, M.
journal: Advances in Medical Technologies and Clinical Practice
abstract: While computer-aided drug research has been in use for a long time, both academia and pharmaceutical corporations have recently experienced a significant change in their use of computational tools. The overwhelming amount of data on ligand properties, their interaction with therapeutic targets, and their three-dimensional structures, along with the availability of powerful computers and the creation of vast virtual libraries containing billions of drug-like molecules, have all played a role in bringing about this transformation. This proposal aims to analyse the latest advancements in ligand discovery technologies, their potential to revolutionise the drug discovery and development process, and the challenges they encounter in the field of medicinal chemistry. In addition, we intend to investigate the rapid and inclusive development of diverse, potent, selective, and drug-like ligands that bind to protein targets. This process aims to democratise drug discovery and create affordable, safer, and more effective small-molecule therapies in the field of medicinal chemistry.
